name: Object Storage Transfer
summary: Plan object-storage transfers between HPC filesystems and cloud remotes.
description: This skill helps users plan and run conservative transfers between HPC filesystems and S3-compatible or cloud object storage remotes using rclone, with dry-run defaults, copy-first behavior, optional sync controls, bounded transfer settings, log capture, and post-transfer validation hooks.

tools.name: aws
tools.notes: Optional AWS CLI fallback for sites that standardize on aws s3 sync.

inputs.name: source_uri
inputs.description: Local path, rclone remote path, or S3 URI to transfer from.

inputs.name: destination_uri
inputs.description: Local path, rclone remote path, or S3 URI to transfer to.

inputs.name: transfer_mode
inputs.description: Transfer mode, normally copy for non-destructive transfers or sync only after delete behavior is reviewed.

inputs.name: run_transfer
inputs.description: Opt-in flag that changes the examples from dry-run planning to actual transfer.

inputs.name: bandwidth_limit
inputs.description: Optional bandwidth limit for shared login nodes, data transfer nodes, or object-storage egress policy.

outputs.name: transfer_plan
outputs.description: Dry-run or executed command with selected mode, source, destination, transfer bounds, and log paths.

outputs.name: transfer_log
outputs.description: rclone or AWS CLI output suitable for review and support handoff.

outputs.name: validation_report
outputs.description: Optional rclone check output or AWS dry-run review notes for confirming expected object movement.

examples.title: Plan or run an rclone object-storage transfer
Plan or run an AWS CLI S3 sync

tests.description: Validate the manifest and referenced examples.

Run the rclone example in dry-run mode against a small user-owned test prefix, review the log and combined report, then set RUN_OBJECT_TRANSFER=1 only after destination and bandwidth policy are confirmed.
